Tennis Automated | ATP Fantasy | Week 5 Draw Update

Week 5 Draw Update Cheat Sheet

Draw update for 20-26 April 2026. Week 5 remains inside the Madrid ATP Masters 1000 event window, which runs 22 April - 3 May, 2026. With the official bracket now in focus, fantasy decisions shift from broad Madrid theory to section shape, advancement paths, Bonus Ball safety, and which roster spots still offer real value after the draw.

Week Window 20-26 April
Slate Madrid only
Surface Clay altitude
Status Draw update edition

Week 5 Draw Lens

What To Lean Into

Once the draw is live, the biggest edge comes from reading path quality correctly. In Madrid, the best fantasy targets are still players with strong overall level and scoring-friendly styles, but bracket shape now decides which favorites are protected, which mid-tier names have room to run, and where a Bonus Ball can be deployed with confidence.

  • Prioritize players with clean early routes and realistic multi-win paths.
  • Use section shape to separate true anchors from overpriced names.
  • Target value plays who avoid immediate collisions with top seeds.
  • Favor players whose serve and first-strike patterns can cash in quickly in Madrid conditions.

What To Fade

The draw update is where lineup mistakes usually become clearer. Dangerous sections, early heavyweight matchups, and crowded value pockets can all reduce fantasy efficiency fast. In a one-event slate, avoiding obvious bracket traps matters almost as much as finding the right upside lane.

  • Do not force expensive names through visibly difficult sections.
  • Be careful with trendy values packed into the same crowded pocket.
  • Avoid Bonus Ball exposure on players with shaky early-round paths.
  • Do not confuse clay comfort with actual draw-based advancement equity.

What Matters After The Draw

ATP Fantasy still runs through the familiar 100-credit build with six starters, two alternates, and one Bonus Ball player scoring double. In a Madrid-only week, the official draw is the key unlock because it turns broad player preference into actual path analysis.

The post-draw edge is simple: identify which players combine talent, fit, and bracket stability. Madrid's altitude clay can still reward aggressive serving and first-strike tennis, but now the sharper question is who gets the cleanest route to stack wins, straight-set bonuses, and ace volume without running into immediate danger.

+2 per ace +10 straight-sets win +20 upset win -2 per double fault -20 upset loss
Draw Update Themes
  • Bracket shape now drives most close fantasy decisions.
  • Bonus Ball choices should start with route safety.
  • Value improves when a player lands in an open section.
  • Crowded quarters can turn good names into weak fantasy plays.

Fantasy Priorities This Week

Best roster construction angle

Build around one or two players with stable advancement outlooks, then use the remaining salary on names whose sections offer realistic paths to multiple wins.

Best scoring angle

Look for players who can pair draw safety with Madrid-friendly scoring traits such as ace upside, hold stability, and straight-sets potential.

Best process angle

Use the official bracket to compare route difficulty, likely early-round resistance, and whether a player's section is opening or congested.

Best caution flag

If a player needs several upset-level wins just to justify salary, that is usually a sign the draw has made them a weaker fantasy investment.

Core Plays After The Draw

Protected premium anchors

The strongest core plays are top-end names who landed in manageable sections and still project for deep-run equity without immediate bracket stress.

Stable mid-tier starters

Mid-range players become core options when the draw gives them a realistic route through the opening rounds and enough scoring upside to beat salary.

Section winners to target

When one pocket of the bracket looks softer than the rest, players positioned to capitalize there deserve extra fantasy attention even if they were only secondary targets pre-draw.

Bonus Ball Board

Best Bonus Ball profile

The ideal multiplier remains a player with both title-level ceiling and a clean enough route to avoid early volatility.

Strong default approach

If one premium favorite clearly has the safest section among the top options, that player is usually the cleanest Bonus Ball starting point.

When to get aggressive

A mid-tier Bonus Ball can work if the bracket opens dramatically around them and their path to several wins looks cleaner than the expensive alternatives.

What to avoid

Do not chase a dangerous quarter just because the ceiling is high. In a one-event slate, early bracket friction can ruin the multiplier spot quickly.

Value Targets

Open-pocket risers

The best values often come from players who landed in sections without immediate elite opposition and now have a clearer path to two or more wins.

Madrid-fit power values

Players outside the premium tier gain appeal when their serve and first-strike patterns align with Madrid conditions and the draw does not force them into instant survival mode.

Alternate stash options

Use alternate spots on live names whose bracket placement gives them real upside if your main lineup needs flexibility closer to lock.

Salary relief with purpose

Cheap plays are only worth it if the draw gives them a believable route to score, not just a recognizable name or surface label.

Traps After The Draw

Crowded-quarter premiums

Even elite players can become poor fantasy values if their section is loaded with dangerous early opponents and little margin for clean scoring.

Overhyped draw winners

Not every player in an open section is automatically a smash play. Make sure the underlying level and scoring profile still support the pick.

Pure clay grinders

Madrid does not always reward slower, attritional fantasy profiles as much as traditional clay assumptions suggest, especially if the bracket offers little straight-sets upside.

Duplicate exposure mistakes

Stacking too many players from the same fragile section can cap lineup upside and increase the chance one bad pocket sinks the build.

Draw update edition for the fantasy week of 20-26 April 2026 within the official ATP Madrid event window of 22 April - 3 May, 2026. Use the bracket to sharpen core plays, Bonus Ball decisions, value targets, and trap avoidance now that the path structure is in view.
